title: Chicken Gyros
subtitle: Spiced grilled chicken in warm pitta with tzatziki
excerpt: Chicken gyros are spiced, marinated chicken thighs grilled until charred and then sliced thin for wrapping in warm pitta. The spice mix of paprika, cumin, and oregano gives the chicken a warm, fragrant crust. Serve with tzatziki, tomatoes, and red onion.
sourceNotes: Gyros (meaning 'turn' in Greek) refers to the rotating vertical spit traditional for this dish. The home version uses chicken thighs marinated in yoghurt and spices, cooked flat on a grill or griddle pan, then sliced. Chicken gyros are one of the most popular street food variations in Greece and across Europe.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Marinate the chicken overnight for a deeper, more complex flavour.

content.text: Chicken gyros is a Greek street food of spiced, marinated chicken thighs cooked over high heat until charred and juicy, then sliced thin and packed into warm pitta with tzatziki and salad. The yoghurt marinade keeps the chicken moist; the paprika and cumin give it colour and warmth.

Mix the yoghurt, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, paprika, cumin, and oregano in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Add the chicken thighs and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Heat a griddle pan over high heat until very hot. Cook the chicken thighs in batches, not overlapping, for 5 to 6 minutes per side until char marks form and the chicken is cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 75°C. Rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the rested chicken into thin strips. Fill warm pitta breads with chicken, sliced tomato, red onion, and tzatziki. A squeeze of lemon is good.

items.symptom: The yoghurt marinade burns on the griddle
items.cause: Heat too high, or marinade not shaken off before cooking
items.fix: Shake or wipe off any thick blobs of marinade before the chicken goes on the pan. A thin coating is fine and helps form the crust.

items.cause: Overcooked, or breast used instead of thigh
items.fix: Boneless thighs are the right cut here; they stay juicy even with high heat. Check at 5 minutes per side.

content.text: Chicken gyros is one of the most popular fast foods in Greece. It is sold from gyradiko shops alongside pork gyros and souvlaki wraps. The home version is a reliable weeknight meal that scales up easily for a crowd: more thighs, more pitta, and a big bowl of tzatziki.
